heading: Excess flow valves
label: 60110 Excess flow valves

children.text: a natural gas distribution system installed after the effective date of regulations prescribed under this section; and
any other natural gas distribution system when repair to the system requires replacing a part to accommodate installing excess flow valves.
the types of customers to which the distribution system supplies gas, including hospitals, schools, and commercial enterprises;
the technical feasibility and cost of installing, operating, and maintaining the valve;
the public safety benefits of installing the valve;
the location of customer meters; and
other factors the Secretary considers relevant.

children.label: A
children.text: the availability of excess flow valves for installation in the system;

children.label: B
children.text: safety benefits to be derived from installation; and

children.label: C
children.text: costs associated with installation, maintenance, and replacement.

The standards shall provide that, except when installation is required under subsection (b) of this section, excess flow valves shall be installed at the request of the customer if the customer will pay all costs associated with installation.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: d .—Report
children.text: If the Secretary decides under subsection (b) of this section that there are no circumstances under which an operator must install excess flow valves, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the reasons for the decision not later than 30 days after the decision is made.

children.heading: .—Performance Standards
children.label: e .—Performance Standards
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall develop standards for the performance of excess flow valves used to protect lines in a natural gas distribution system. The Secretary may adopt industry accepted performance standards in order to comply with the requirement under the preceding sentence. The standards shall be incorporated into regulations the Secretary prescribes under this section. All excess flow valves shall be installed according to the standards.April 24, 1994
